pi_items.source: Subjective label with no objective verification mechanism.
Matches verifier.py docstring and task.json prompt text.
verifier.py maximum possible score sums to 100.
Matches task.json requirements and explicit metric checking in export_result.sh and verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes 'SweetHome3DExample7.sh3d' (Contemporary Villa), an official real-world sample file from the sweethome3d.com gallery. The setup script modifies it by stripping out all <pieceOfFurniture> and <furnitureGroup> tags to create an empty shell, but fails to strip <doorOrWindow> and <room> tags, unintentionally leaving 30 doors/windows and 19 rooms (8 with floor colors) in the starter file. Furthermore, export_result.sh contains a critical defect: it attempts to count new doors/windows by looking for '<pieceOfFurniture doorOrWindow=

>' elements instead of the correct '<doorOrWindow>' tags used by Sweet Home 3D XML. Consequently, 'new_doors' permanently evaluates to 0, making Criterion 3 impossible to pass. Conversely, Criterion 5 checks the absolute count of rooms (19) and colored floors (8) rather than new additions, which automatically awards 10 free points to any agent submission without any required actions.
